title: MrBeast Editor Enforcement
subject: Artem Kaptur
summary: A MrBeast editor was sanctioned after trading on video-related markets with unusually accurate low-probability bets tied to insider production knowledge.
notes: Kalshi publicly cited the case as a major example of insider-trading surveillance.

title: Candidate Self-Trading Case
subject: California governor candidate
summary: A candidate allegedly traded on his own candidacy, producing a clear conflict-of-interest pattern detectable through public reporting and trade timing.
notes: The trader publicly referenced the activity, which accelerated detection.

title: Maduro Capture Windfall
subject: Anonymous trader
summary: A trader reportedly made roughly $400K on capture markets shortly before public reporting, suggesting a sharp timing-information advantage.
penalty: No platform penalty publicly recorded here
notes: Included as a comparable cross-platform case for prediction-market surveillance.

title: Military Operations Betting Arrests
subject: Several unnamed bettors
summary: Authorities arrested participants accused of using classified military information to place prediction-market bets ahead of public events.
notes: Represents the national-security extreme of the same surveillance problem.

title: Giannis Conflict-of-Interest Scrutiny
subject: Giannis Antetokounmpo
summary: A major athlete allegedly became financially tied to the platform amid heavy wagering on his own future, raising governance and fairness concerns.
penalty: No penalty recorded in the seed data
notes: This is primarily a governance case, not a direct confirmed trade-surveillance case.
